{{Short description|Physical field produced by electrically charged objects}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Electromagnetic field
| image = Onde electromagnétique.png
| caption = An [[electromagnetic wave]] propagating through space: the [[electric field]] (red) and [[magnetic field]] (blue) oscillate perpendicular to each other and to the direction of propagation.
| topics = [[Electric field]], [[Magnetic field]], [[Maxwell's equations]], [[Electromagnetic wave]]
}}

An '''electromagnetic field''' ('''EM field''' or '''EMF''') is a physical [[field (physics)|field]] produced by electrically charged objects. It affects the behaviour of charged objects in the vicinity of the field. The electromagnetic field extends infinitely throughout [[space]] and consists of two inseparable components: the [[electric field]] <math>\mathbf{E}</math> and the [[magnetic field]] <math>\mathbf{B}</math>.

== Mathematical description ==
The electromagnetic field is fully described by [[Maxwell's equations]]:

:<math>\nabla \cdot \mathbf{E} = \frac{\rho}{\varepsilon_0}</math>
:<math>\nabla \cdot \mathbf{B} = 0</math>
:<math>\nabla \times \mathbf{E} = -\frac{\partial\mathbf{B}}{\partial t}</math>
:<math>\nabla \times \mathbf{B} = \mu_0\mathbf{J} + \mu_0\varepsilon_0\frac{\partial\mathbf{E}}{\partial t}</math>

== Field tensor ==
In [[special relativity]], the electric and magnetic fields unify into the [[electromagnetic field tensor]] <math>F^{\mu\nu}</math>:

:<math>F^{\mu\nu} = \begin{pmatrix}
0 & -E_x/c & -E_y/c & -E_z/c \\
E_x/c & 0 & -B_z & B_y \\
E_y/c & B_z & 0 & -B_x \\
E_z/c & -B_y & B_x & 0
\end{pmatrix}</math>

== Energy and momentum ==
The [[energy density]] of the electromagnetic field is:
:<math>u = \frac{1}{2}\left(\varepsilon_0 E^2 + \frac{1}{\mu_0}B^2\right)</math>

The [[Poynting vector]] describes the energy flux:
:<math>\mathbf{S} = \frac{1}{\mu_0}\mathbf{E} \times \mathbf{B}</math>

== Quantum description ==
In [[quantum electrodynamics]], the electromagnetic field is quantized, and its excitations correspond to [[photon]]s. Forces between charged particles are mediated by virtual photon exchange.

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Electric field]]
* [[Electromagnetic wave]]
* [[Magnetic field]]
* [[Maxwell's equations]]
* [[Poynting vector]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Electromagnetic field| ]]
[[Category:Electromagnetism]]
[[Category:Concepts in physics]]
